name: experiment-lifecycle
description: Run GPU experiments like a researcher — the full lifecycle in three skills. design-experiment: propose with taste, write a DESIGN.md data-collection spec (what reliable, comparable data this produces + what it's designed to inform — not a pre-registered verdict), clear it with the human through verify-claims gates, emit a thin START.md brief + CHECKLIST.md gates, dispatch to a fresh-context executor. run-experiment: the zero-context executor — arm an independent self-wake for autonomous detached runs, acquire compute (gpu-job), provision + drive + collect + close (RESULTS describes the data per the DESIGN spec, cross-family audit, teardown). log-experiment: log a finished experiment, a design-stage pre-registration, or a note to the research repo as a GATED pull request and merge it — classified by the registry convention (experiment → verify the close-audit; design-stage → verify the design-audit + secret scan; note → deterministic secret scan), cross-family bot approval satisfies branch protection. Instance specifics live in an execution profile; pairs with gpu-job (backend) and verify-claims (gates).
